git-forensics.description: Git archaeology -- find secrets, trace bugs, understand history

commands.comment: find every commit that added or removed a string (pickaxe search)
deleted files -- credentials often get 'removed' but live in history
search commit messages -- find when a fix or feature was introduced

commands.cmd: git log --format='%H' --all | xargs git grep -l '$SEARCH_STRING' 2>/dev/null | sort -u
commands.comment: brute-force search across all history -- finds buried secrets

commands.cmd: git rev-list --objects --all | git cat-file --batch-check='%(objecttype) %(objectname) %(objectsize) %(rest)' | awk '/^blob/ {print $3,$4}' | sort -rn | head -20
commands.comment: largest blobs ever committed -- binaries, data dumps, vendor dirs
top contributors by commit count -- who knows this code best
timeline of changes in a date range -- incident correlation
